customBuild.title: Custom build (OpenShift only)
customBuild.description: Custom build configuration for the DAMAP backend.

backend.title: DAMAP backend custom build
backend.description: Custom build configuration for the DAMAP backend

name.title: Name
name.description: Set this field to non-empty to trigger an OpenShift build for the DAMAP backend

repo.title: Repository
repo.description: Git repository to build the DAMAP backend from

ref.title: Ref
ref.description: Git ref for the DAMAP backend build

frontend.title: DAMAP frontend custom build
frontend.description: Custom build configuration for the DAMAP frontend

name.title: Name
name.description: Set this field to non-empty to trigger an OpenShift build for the DAMAP frontend

repo.title: Repository
repo.description: Git repository to build the DAMAP frontend from

ref.title: Ref
ref.description: Git ref for the DAMAP frontend build

customDomain.title: Custom domain
customDomain.description: Use a custom domain for the ingress. You have to configure the DNS records for the custom domain yourself.

dbName.title: Database name
dbName.description: Database name for the PostgreSQL server.

hostFolder.title: Host folder
hostFolder.description: Host folder for persistence (only for demo/small scale deployments, not for production)

ingressClass.title: Ingress class
ingressClass.description: Ingress class to use for the DAMAP ingress. If empty, the default ingress class will be used.

autoCreateDatabases.title: Auto-create databases
autoCreateDatabases.description: Automatically create a new database for each tenant. This is required if multitenancy is enabled, but you can disable it if you want to manage the databases manually.

tenants.title: Tenants
tenants.description: List of tenants to create. The tenant name will be used as the database name.

persistData.title: Persist data
persistData.description: Persist data across deployments. This will create a Persistent Volume (Vanilla K8s) and Persistent Volume Claim for the database. Do not use for production without proper backup and migration strategy!

personServices.title: Person services
personServices.description: Configuration for person services that DAMAP can use to fetch person data. The services will be used in the order they are defined here.

storageClassName.title: Storage class name
storageClassName.description: Name for the storage class to use for allocating PVs.

storageSize.title: Storage size
storageSize.description: Storage size for the Persistent Volume Claim if persistData is true.

tlsClusterIssuer.title: TLS cluster issuer
tlsClusterIssuer.description: Cluster issuer to use for TLS certificates.

useExistingSecret.title: Use existing secret
useExistingSecret.description: Use an existing Kubernetes secret for the database credentials instead of creating a new one. This is useful if you want to use Sealed Secrets or manage the secret manually.

useHostFolder.title: Use host folder
useHostFolder.description: Use a host folder for persistence (only for demo/small scale deployments, not for production)

frontend.title: Frontend
frontend.description: Frontend customization settings. The two logos must not exceed 1 MB in size together.

logo.title: Logo (authenticated)
logo.description: Logo for the authenticated area in SVG format.

logo_cropped.title: Logo (front page)
logo_cropped.description: Logo for the front page in SVG format.

customDomain.title: Custom domain
customDomain.description: Use a custom domain for the ingress. You have to configure the DNS records for the custom domain yourself.

dbName.title: Database name
dbName.description: Database name for the PostgreSQL server.

deploy.title: Deploy
deploy.description: Co-deploy Keycloak. Not for production use.

hostFolder.title: Host folder
hostFolder.description: Host folder for persistence (only for demo/small scale deployments, not for production)

ingressClass.title: Ingress class
ingressClass.description: Ingress class to use for the Keycloak ingress. If empty, the default ingress class will be used.

persistData.title: Persist data
persistData.description: Persist data across deployments. This will create a Persistent Volume (Vanilla K8s) and Persistent Volume Claim for the database. Do not use for production without proper backup and migration strategy!

storageClassName.title: Storage class name
storageClassName.description: Name for the storage class to use for allocating PVs.

storageSize.title: Storage size
storageSize.description: Storage size for the Persistent Volume Claim if persistData is true.

tlsClusterIssuer.title: TLS cluster issuer
tlsClusterIssuer.description: Cluster issuer to use for TLS certificates.

useExistingSecret.title: Use existing secret
useExistingSecret.description: Use an existing Kubernetes secret for the database credentials instead of creating a new one. This is useful if you want to use Sealed Secrets or manage the secret manually.

useHostFolder.title: Use host folder
useHostFolder.description: Use a host folder for persistence (only for demo/small scale deployments, not for production)

adminRoleName.title: Admin Role Name
adminRoleName.description: The name of the admin role in the OIDC server.

affiliationsClaim.title: Affiliations claim
affiliationsClaim.description: The name of the claim in the OIDC token that contains the user's affiliations. This is used to determine which tenants the user has access to.

clientId.title: OIDC client ID
clientId.description: The OIDC client ID as configured in the OIDC server. This should match the client ID configured in Keycloak if keycloak.deploy is true.

emailClaim.title: Email claim
emailClaim.description: The name of the claim in the OIDC token that contains the user's email.

familyNameClaim.title: Family name claim
familyNameClaim.description: The name of the claim in the OIDC token that contains the user's family name.

givenNameClaim.title: Given name claim
givenNameClaim.description: The name of the claim in the OIDC token that contains the user's given name.

issuer.title: OIDC issuer
issuer.description: The endpoint the DAMAP frontend should use from the browser. This should be accessible from the user's computer. This setting will be ignored if keycloak.deploy is true.

nameClaim.title: Name claim
nameClaim.description: The name of the claim in the OIDC token that contains the user's name.

serverUrl.title: Backend OIDC endpoint
serverUrl.description: The endpoint the DAMAP backend should use to validate tokens and fetch user info. This should be accessible from the cluster. This setting will be ignored if keycloak.deploy is true.

userIdClaim.title: User ID claim
userIdClaim.description: The name of the claim in the OIDC token that contains the user's ID.

userRolesClaimPath.title: User roles claim path
userRolesClaimPath.description: The path to the claim in the OIDC token that contains the user's roles.

apiKey.title: API key
apiKey.description: API key for authenticating with the Pure API. This is required if backend is set to 'http'.

backend.title: Backend
backend.description: The backend to use for fetching data from Elsevier Pure. If 'file' is selected, the data will be read from the 'personsData' and 'projectsData' fields in this configuration. If 'http' is selected, the data will be fetched from the Pure API using the provided endpoint and API key.

descriptionClassification.title: Description classification
descriptionClassification.description: The classification in Pure that contains the description for persons and projects. This should match the classification used in Pure for the description.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Enable the Elsevier Pure integration. If false, the Pure integration will be disabled and the backend will not attempt to fetch data from Pure.

endpoint.title: Endpoint
endpoint.description: The base URL for the Pure API. This should be the URL of your Pure instance followed by '/api', e.g., 'https://your-pure-instance.com/api'. This is required if backend is set to 'http'.

personsData.title: PURE persons data
personsData.description: Mock persons data to use when backend is set to 'file'. This should be structured the same way as the response from the Pure API for fetching persons, including the 'count', 'pageInformation', and 'items' fields. The 'items' field should be an array of person objects as returned by the Pure API.

projectLeadClassification.title: Project lead classification
projectLeadClassification.description: The classification in Pure that indicates the project lead role. This should match the classification used in Pure for the project lead role.

projectsData.title: PURE Projects Data
projectsData.description: Mock projects data to use when backend is set to 'file'. This should be structured the same way as the response from the Pure API for fetching projects, including the 'count', 'pageInformation', and 'items' fields. The 'items' field should be an array of project objects as returned by the Pure API.

roleClassifications.title: Role classifications
roleClassifications.description: Mappings from Pure classifications to DAMAP roles. The keys should be the Pure classifications used for roles in your Pure instance, and the values should be the corresponding DAMAP role values. This is used to determine which DAMAP role to assign to a person based on their classification in Pure. The DAMAP role values should be one of the following: DATA_COLLECTOR, DATA_CURATOR, DATA_MANAGER, DISTRIBUTOR, EDITOR, HOSTING_INSTITUTION, PRODUCER, PROJECT_LEADER, PROJECT_MANAGER, PROJECT_MEMBER, REGISTRATION_AGENCY, REGISTRATION_AUTHORITY, RELATED_PERSON, RESEARCHER, RESEARCH_GROUP, RIGHTS_HOLDER, SPONSOR, SUPERVISOR, WORK_PACKAGE_LEADER, PRINCIPAL_INVESTIGATOR, PROJECT_COORDINATOR, OTHER.
